global-advisories.title: List global security advisories

parameters.name: ghsa_id
parameters.description: If specified, only advisories with this GHSA (GitHub Security Advisory) identifier will be returned.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: If specified, only advisories of this type will be returned. By default, a request with no other parameters defined will only return reviewed advisories that are not malware.

parameters.name: cve_id
parameters.description: If specified, only advisories with this CVE (Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures) identifier will be returned.

parameters.name: ecosystem
parameters.description: If specified, only advisories for these ecosystems will be returned.

schema.description: The package's language or package management ecosystem.

parameters.name: severity
parameters.description: If specified, only advisories with these severities will be returned.

parameters.name: cwes
parameters.description:
If specified, only advisories with these Common Weakness Enumerations (CWEs) will be returned.

Example: cwes=79,284,22 or cwes[]=79&cwes[]=284&cwes[]=22

parameters.name: is_withdrawn
parameters.description: Whether to only return advisories that have been withdrawn.

parameters.name: affects
parameters.description:
If specified, only return advisories that affect any of package or package@version. A maximum of 1000 packages can be specified. If the query parameter causes the URL to exceed the maximum URL length supported by your client, you must specify fewer packages.

Example: affects=package1,package2@1.0.0,package3@2.0.0 or affects[]=package1&affects[]=package2@1.0.0

parameters.name: published
parameters.description:
If specified, only return advisories that were published on a date or date range.

For more information on the syntax of the date range, see "Understanding the search syntax."

parameters.name: updated
parameters.description:
If specified, only return advisories that were updated on a date or date range.

For more information on the syntax of the date range, see "Understanding the search syntax."

parameters.name: modified
parameters.description:
If specified, only show advisories that were updated or published on a date or date range.

For more information on the syntax of the date range, see "Understanding the search syntax."

parameters.name: epss_percentage
parameters.description: If specified, only return advisories that have an EPSS percentage score that matches the provided value. The EPSS percentage represents the likelihood of a CVE being exploited.

parameters.name: epss_percentile
parameters.description: If specified, only return advisories that have an EPSS percentile score that matches the provided value. The EPSS percentile represents the relative rank of the CVE's likelihood of being exploited compared to other CVEs.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results before this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results after this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: The direction to sort the results by.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: The property to sort the results by.

global-advisories.descriptionHTML:
Lists all global security advisories that match the specified parameters. If no other parameters are defined, the request will return only GitHub-reviewed advisories that are not malware.

By default, all responses will exclude advisories for malware, because malware are not standard vulnerabilities. To list advisories for malware, you must include the type parameter in your request, with the value malware. For more information about the different types of security advisories, see "About the GitHub Advisory database."

example.description: This bug allows an attacker to read portions of the affected server’s memory, potentially disclosing sensitive information.

items.description: A GitHub Security Advisory.

cve_id.description: The Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVE) ID.

url.description: The API URL for the advisory.

repository_advisory_url.description: The API URL for the repository advisory.

summary.description: A short summary of the advisory.

description.description: A detailed description of what the advisory entails.

type.description: The type of advisory.

source_code_location.description: The URL of the advisory's source code.

type.description: The type of identifier.

items.description: URLs with more information regarding the advisory.

published_at.description: The date and time of when the advisory was published, in ISO 8601 format.

updated_at.description: The date and time of when the advisory was last updated, in ISO 8601 format.

github_reviewed_at.description: The date and time of when the advisory was reviewed by GitHub, in ISO 8601 format.

nvd_published_at.description:
The date and time when the advisory was published in the National Vulnerability Database, in ISO 8601 format.
This field is only populated when the advisory is imported from the National Vulnerability Database.

withdrawn_at.description: The date and time of when the advisory was withdrawn, in ISO 8601 format.

vulnerabilities.description: The products and respective version ranges affected by the advisory.

items.description: A vulnerability describing the product and its affected versions within a GitHub Security Advisory.

package.description: The name of the package affected by the vulnerability.

ecosystem.description: The package's language or package management ecosystem.

name.description: The unique package name within its ecosystem.

vulnerable_version_range.description: The range of the package versions affected by the vulnerability.

first_patched_version.description: The package version that resolves the vulnerability.

vulnerable_functions.description: The functions in the package that are affected by the vulnerability.

epss.description: The EPSS scores as calculated by the [Exploit Prediction Scoring System](https://www.first.org/epss).

credits.description: The users who contributed to the advisory.

type.description: The type of credit the user is receiving.

statusCodes.description: Validation failed, or the endpoint has been spammed.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: The direction to sort the results by.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: The property to sort the results by.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results before this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results after this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of advisories to return per page. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Filter by the state of the repository advisories. Only advisories of this state will be returned.

repository-advisories.descriptionHTML:
Lists repository security advisories for an organization.

The authenticated user must be an owner or security manager for the organization to use this endpoint.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo or repository_advisories:write scope to use this endpoint.

repository-advisories.title: List repository security advisories

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: The direction to sort the results by.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: The property to sort the results by.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results before this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results after this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of advisories to return per page. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Filter by state of the repository advisories. Only advisories of this state will be returned.

repository-advisories.descriptionHTML:
Lists security advisories in a repository.

The authenticated user can access unpublished security advisories from a repository if they are a security manager or administrator of that repository, or if they are a collaborator on any security advisory.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo or repository_advisories:read scope to to get a published security advisory in a private repository, or any unpublished security advisory that the authenticated user has access to.

repository-advisories.title: Create a repository security advisory

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

bodyParameters.name: summary
bodyParameters.description: A short summary of the advisory.

bodyParameters.name: description
bodyParameters.description: A detailed description of what the advisory impacts.

bodyParameters.name: cve_id
bodyParameters.description: The Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVE) ID.

bodyParameters.name: vulnerabilities
bodyParameters.description: A product affected by the vulnerability detailed in a repository security advisory.

childParamsGroups.name: package
childParamsGroups.description: The name of the package affected by the vulnerability.

childParamsGroups.name: ecosystem
childParamsGroups.description: The package's language or package management ecosystem.

childParamsGroups.name: name
childParamsGroups.description: The unique package name within its ecosystem.

childParamsGroups.name: vulnerable_version_range
childParamsGroups.description: The range of the package versions affected by the vulnerability.

childParamsGroups.name: patched_versions
childParamsGroups.description: The package version(s) that resolve the vulnerability.

childParamsGroups.name: vulnerable_functions
childParamsGroups.description: The functions in the package that are affected.

bodyParameters.name: cwe_ids
bodyParameters.description: A list of Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) IDs.

bodyParameters.name: credits
bodyParameters.description: A list of users receiving credit for their participation in the security advisory.

childParamsGroups.name: login
childParamsGroups.description: The username of the user credited.

childParamsGroups.name: type
childParamsGroups.description: The type of credit the user is receiving.

bodyParameters.name: severity
bodyParameters.description: The severity of the advisory. You must choose between setting this field or cvss_vector_string.

bodyParameters.name: cvss_vector_string
bodyParameters.description: The CVSS vector that calculates the severity of the advisory. You must choose between setting this field or severity.

bodyParameters.name: start_private_fork
bodyParameters.description: Whether to create a temporary private fork of the repository to collaborate on a fix.

repository-advisories.descriptionHTML:
Creates a new repository security advisory.

In order to create a draft repository security advisory, the authenticated user must be a security manager or administrator of that repository.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo or repository_advisories:write scope to use this endpoint.

bodyParameters.description: A more in-depth description of what the problem is.

repository-advisories.title: Get a repository security advisory

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: ghsa_id
parameters.description: The GHSA (GitHub Security Advisory) identifier of the advisory.

repository-advisories.descriptionHTML:
Get a repository security advisory using its GitHub Security Advisory (GHSA) identifier.

Anyone can access any published security advisory on a public repository.

The authenticated user can access an unpublished security advisory from a repository if they are a security manager or administrator of that repository, or if they are a collaborator on the security advisory.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo or repository_advisories:read scope to to get a published security advisory in a private repository, or any unpublished security advisory that the authenticated user has access to.

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: ghsa_id
parameters.description: The GHSA (GitHub Security Advisory) identifier of the advisory.

bodyParameters.name: summary
bodyParameters.description: A short summary of the advisory.

bodyParameters.name: description
bodyParameters.description: A detailed description of what the advisory impacts.

bodyParameters.name: cve_id
bodyParameters.description: The Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVE) ID.

bodyParameters.name: vulnerabilities
bodyParameters.description: A product affected by the vulnerability detailed in a repository security advisory.

childParamsGroups.name: patched_versions
childParamsGroups.description: The package version(s) that resolve the vulnerability.

childParamsGroups.name: vulnerable_functions
childParamsGroups.description: The functions in the package that are affected.

bodyParameters.name: cwe_ids
bodyParameters.description: A list of Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) IDs.

bodyParameters.name: credits
bodyParameters.description: A list of users receiving credit for their participation in the security advisory.

childParamsGroups.name: login
childParamsGroups.description: The username of the user credited.

childParamsGroups.name: type
childParamsGroups.description: The type of credit the user is receiving.

bodyParameters.name: severity
bodyParameters.description: The severity of the advisory. You must choose between setting this field or cvss_vector_string.

bodyParameters.name: cvss_vector_string
bodyParameters.description: The CVSS vector that calculates the severity of the advisory. You must choose between setting this field or severity.

bodyParameters.name: state
bodyParameters.description: The state of the advisory.

bodyParameters.name: collaborating_users
bodyParameters.description: A list of usernames who have been granted write access to the advisory.

bodyParameters.name: collaborating_teams
bodyParameters.description: A list of team slugs which have been granted write access to the advisory.

repository-advisories.descriptionHTML:
Update a repository security advisory using its GitHub Security Advisory (GHSA) identifier.

In order to update any security advisory, the authenticated user must be a security manager or administrator of that repository, or a collaborator on the repository security advisory.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo or repository_advisories:write scope to use this endpoint.

enterprise_id.description: Unique identifier of the enterprise to which this team belongs

private_fork.description: A temporary private fork of the advisory's repository for collaborating on a fix.

id.description: A unique identifier of the repository.

node_id.description: The GraphQL identifier of the repository.

full_name.description: The full, globally unique, name of the repository.

html_url.description: The URL to view the repository on GitHub.com.

fork.description: Whether the repository is a fork.

url.description: The URL to get more information about the repository from the GitHub API.

archive_url.description: A template for the API URL to download the repository as an archive.

assignees_url.description: A template for the API URL to list the available assignees for issues in the repository.

blobs_url.description: A template for the API URL to create or retrieve a raw Git blob in the repository.

branches_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about branches in the repository.

collaborators_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about collaborators of the repository.

comments_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about comments on the repository.

commits_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about commits on the repository.

compare_url.description: A template for the API URL to compare two commits or refs.

contents_url.description: A template for the API URL to get the contents of the repository.

contributors_url.description: A template for the API URL to list the contributors to the repository.

deployments_url.description: The API URL to list the deployments of the repository.

downloads_url.description: The API URL to list the downloads on the repository.

events_url.description: The API URL to list the events of the repository.

forks_url.description: The API URL to list the forks of the repository.

git_commits_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about Git commits of the repository.

git_refs_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about Git refs of the repository.

git_tags_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about Git tags of the repository.

issue_comment_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about issue comments on the repository.

issue_events_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about issue events on the repository.

issues_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about issues on the repository.

keys_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about deploy keys on the repository.

labels_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about labels of the repository.

languages_url.description: The API URL to get information about the languages of the repository.

merges_url.description: The API URL to merge branches in the repository.

milestones_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about milestones of the repository.

notifications_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about notifications on the repository.

pulls_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about pull requests on the repository.

releases_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about releases on the repository.

stargazers_url.description: The API URL to list the stargazers on the repository.

statuses_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about statuses of a commit.

subscribers_url.description: The API URL to list the subscribers on the repository.

subscription_url.description: The API URL to subscribe to notifications for this repository.

tags_url.description: The API URL to get information about tags on the repository.

teams_url.description: The API URL to list the teams on the repository.

trees_url.description: A template for the API URL to create or retrieve a raw Git tree of the repository.

hooks_url.description: The API URL to list the hooks on the repository.

statusCodes.description: Validation failed, or the endpoint has been spammed.

repository-advisories.title: Request a CVE for a repository security advisory

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: ghsa_id
parameters.description: The GHSA (GitHub Security Advisory) identifier of the advisory.

repository-advisories.descriptionHTML:
If you want a CVE identification number for the security vulnerability in your project, and don't already have one, you can request a CVE identification number from GitHub. For more information see "Requesting a CVE identification number."

You may request a CVE for public repositories, but cannot do so for private repositories.

In order to request a CVE for a repository security advisory, the authenticated user must be a security manager or administrator of that repository.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo or repository_advisories:write scope to use this endpoint.

statusCodes.description: Validation failed, or the endpoint has been spammed.

repository-advisories.title: Create a temporary private fork

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: ghsa_id
parameters.description: The GHSA (GitHub Security Advisory) identifier of the advisory.

repository-advisories.descriptionHTML:
Create a temporary private fork to collaborate on fixing a security vulnerability in your repository.

Note

Forking a repository happens asynchronously. You may have to wait up to 5 minutes before you can access the fork.

size.description: The size of the repository, in kilobytes. Size is calculated hourly. When a repository is initially created, the size is 0.

pull_request_creation_policy.description: The policy controlling who can create pull requests: all or collaborators_only.

disabled.description: Returns whether or not this repository disabled.

visibility.description: The repository visibility: public, private, or internal.

private.description: Whether the repository is private or public.

size.description: The size of the repository, in kilobytes. Size is calculated hourly. When a repository is initially created, the size is 0.

default_branch.description: The default branch of the repository.

is_template.description: Whether this repository acts as a template that can be used to generate new repositories.

pull_request_creation_policy.description: The policy controlling who can create pull requests: all or collaborators_only.

disabled.description: Returns whether or not this repository disabled.

visibility.description: The repository visibility: public, private, or internal.

allow_rebase_merge.description: Whether to allow rebase merges for pull requests.

allow_squash_merge.description: Whether to allow squash merges for pull requests.

allow_auto_merge.description: Whether to allow Auto-merge to be used on pull requests.

delete_branch_on_merge.description: Whether to delete head branches when pull requests are merged

allow_update_branch.description: Whether or not a pull request head branch that is behind its base branch can always be updated even if it is not required to be up to date before merging.

use_squash_pr_title_as_default.description: Whether a squash merge commit can use the pull request title as default. **This property is closing down. Please use `squash_merge_commit_title` instead.

squash_merge_commit_title.description:
The default value for a squash merge commit title:

- `PR_TITLE` - default to the pull request's title.
- `COMMIT_OR_PR_TITLE` - default to the commit's title (if only one commit) or the pull request's title (when more than one commit).

squash_merge_commit_message.description:
The default value for a squash merge commit message:

- `PR_BODY` - default to the pull request's body.
- `COMMIT_MESSAGES` - default to the branch's commit messages.
- `BLANK` - default to a blank commit message.

merge_commit_title.description:
The default value for a merge commit title.

- `PR_TITLE` - default to the pull request's title.
- `MERGE_MESSAGE` - default to the classic title for a merge message (e.g., Merge pull request #123 from branch-name).

merge_commit_message.description:
The default value for a merge commit message.

- `PR_TITLE` - default to the pull request's title.
- `PR_BODY` - default to the pull request's body.
- `BLANK` - default to a blank commit message.

advanced_security.description:
Enable or disable GitHub Advanced Security for the repository.

For standalone Code Scanning or Secret Protection products, this parameter cannot be used.
